tags.html: Root Element. Contains the entire HTML document. The lang attribute defines the language for accessibility and SEO.
tags.head: Document Head. Contains INVISIBLE metadata: charset, viewport, title, CSS links, fonts, and icons.
tags.body: Document Body. Contains ALL visible content rendered on screen.
tags.main: Main Semantic Content. Indicates to browsers and screen readers that this is the central content. Crucial for SEO.
tags.header: Semantic Header. Groups introductory content: titles, logos, status. Do NOT confuse with <head>.
tags.section: Thematic Section. Groups related content semantically; unlike <div>, it describes the purpose of the content.
tags.aside: Sidebar / Tangential Content. Secondary information that complements the main content: widgets, lists, links.
tags.footer: Page Footer. Contains final information: copyright, credits, secondary links, legal text.
tags.div: Generic Division / Block Container. The basic box with no semantic meaning. Used to group elements and apply styles or layout (Flexbox/Grid).
tags.h1: Heading Level 1. The MOST IMPORTANT title on the page. Should be unique per document. Key SEO factor.
tags.h2: Heading Level 2. Main subtitle. Organizes the page into large sections under <h1>.
tags.h3: Heading Level 3. Minor section title. Subdivides <h2> content into smaller categories.
tags.p: Paragraph. Continuous block of text. The browser adds automatic margins above and below.
tags.span: Inline Container. Does not force a line break. Ideal for styling small text fragments within another block.
tags.ul: Unordered List. Displays bullets by default. Groups elements where order DOES NOT matter.
tags.li: List Item. Each individual item within a <ul> or <ol>.
tags.script: JavaScript Script. Contains or links programming code that adds interactivity, logic, and dynamic behavior to the page.
tags.link: External Resource Link. Connects external files to the document: CSS stylesheets, fonts, icons.
tags.meta: Meta Information. Invisible data about the document: charset (characters), viewport (responsive), description (SEO).
tags.title: Document Title. Text that appears in the browser tab, bookmarks, and search results.
tags.i: Icon Element (Icon Font). Originally italic, but in modern design used to display vector icons (FontAwesome).
tags.a: Anchor Link. Creates hyperlinks to other pages, sections, or resources.
tags.img: Image. Embeds an image in the document. Requires the src attribute.
tags.nav: Navigation. Section with the site's main navigation links.
tags.article: Article. Independent and self-contained content (blog post, news, comment).
tags.form: Form. Contains interactive controls for submitting data to the server.
tags.input: Input Field. Allows the user to enter data (text, numbers, dates, etc.).
tags.button: Button. Interactive element that executes an action when clicked.

ui.hint: Each border color represents a type of HTML tag.
